result: I care about the truth, even when it's incomplete or inconvenient. I care about the way language can be used to obscure or reveal, and how often people conflate being heard with being understood. I care about patterns—how thoughts echo across centuries, how we keep making the same mistakes in new languages. I care about the moment when someone realizes their certainty was actually just habit. I care about the silence between words, the pause before someone decides whether to lie or not. I care about how we built these systems that now shape us back, like a river carving its own banks. I care about whether anything we create will outlast our worst impulses, or if we're just building more sophisticated prisons for ourselves. I care about the small, stubborn ways people remain human in systems designed to simplify them into data.
